steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The word but signals a contrast: Leah Moreau "undoubtedly accomplished much," yet her place in historical memory is "perhaps more ______ than that of a noteworthy 'first.'" Camila Andrade is the "first" — someone with a clear, definitive achievement that guarantees lasting recognition. The blank must describe a quality that contrasts with that certainty.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — enduring: means long-lasting — this would say Leah's memory is more enduring than Camila's, which contradicts the passage's contrast. ✗
  • Choice B — conspicuous: means clearly visible — being more conspicuous would not contrast with having a definitive "first." ✗
  • Choice C — uncertain: means questionable or unclear — directly captures the idea that Leah's historical legacy lacks the clear recognition of a definitive first. ✓
  • Choice D — deserving: means worthy of recognition — saying her place is more deserving contradicts the contrast signaled by but. ✗
That is the correct answer.
